descriptions: The Beland family took over international trafficking after the Ginsberg-Kayne-Gordon syndicate was arrested.
edges.evidence_quote: After the Federal Bureau of Narcotics under Harry J. Anslinger arrested the Texas-based Ginsberg-Kayne-Gordon criminal syndicate in 1936, the Belands were able to take over international trafficking.

State Line Mob.description: An association of criminal elements operating in the 1950s and 1960s along the Mississippi-Tennessee state line. Involved in bootlegging, gambling, prostitution, robbery, and murder. Owned motels, restaurants, and clubs used for vice. Gained national attention for its feud with Sheriff Buford Pusser, inspiring the film Walking Tall.

descriptions: The Dixie Mafia was strongly connected to the State Line Mob.
edges.evidence_quote: The Dixie Mafia was strongly connected to the State Line Mob and its leader, Carl Douglas White.

descriptions: Dixie Mafia was initially allied with State Line Mob.
edges.evidence_quote: Inizialmente alleata con la piccola associazione criminale State Line Mob

Muerte a Secuestradores.description: Colombian paramilitary group and private army formed in 1981 by drug cartels, politicians, and landowners to fight kidnapping and guerrilla groups. Supported by the Medellín Cartel, it assassinated political opponents and community organizers. Active through the 1990s, it waged counterinsurgency against FARC-EP and M-19.

descriptions: M-19's kidnapping of Nieves Ochoa led to creation of MAS by Medellín cartel to target M-19.
edges.evidence_quote: The kidnapping led to the creation of the MAS/ Muerte a Secuestradores ("Death to Kidnappers") paramilitary group by the Medellin cartel.

descriptions: M-19 listed MAS as an opponent in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Muerte a Secuestradores

descriptions: Cali Cartel co-founded MAS with Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: the Medellín and Cali cartels, as well as associated traffickers, formed the group Muerte a Secuestradores

descriptions: Cali Cartel co-founded and participated in MAS.
edges.evidence_quote: members of the Medellín Cartel, Cali Cartel, the Colombian military, ... came together ... and formed a paramilitary organization known as Muerte a Secuestradores

descriptions: Cali Cartel co-founded MAS with Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: The two cartels participated in other joint ventures in later years, such as the founding of Muerte a Secuestradores (MAS)

descriptions: Medellín Cartel co-founded and participated in MAS.
edges.evidence_quote: members of the Medellín Cartel, Cali Cartel, the Colombian military, ... came together ... and formed a paramilitary organization known as Muerte a Secuestradores

descriptions: The Medellín Cartel created the MAS as its private army.
edges.evidence_quote: De esta manera nació el grupo llamado Muerte a Secuestradores (MAS), un ejército privado de más de 2.230 hombres.

descriptions: MAS waged counterinsurgency warfare against M-19.
edges.evidence_quote: Muerte a Secuestradores assassinated political opponents and community organizers, and waged counterinsurgency warfare against guerrilla movements such as the FARC-EP and the M-19.

edges.target: Asociación Campesina de Ganaderos y Agricultores del Magdalena Medio
descriptions: ACDEGAM provided legal front and logistics for MAS, and MAS backed up ACDEGAM's threats with violence.
edges.evidence_quote: ACDEGAM worked to promote anti-labor policies, and threatened anyone involved with organizing for labor or peasants' rights. The threats were backed up by the MAS, which would come in and attack or assassinate anyone who was suspected of being a 'subversive'.

edges.target: Asociación Campesina de Ganaderos y Agricultores del Magdalena Medio
descriptions: MAS backed ACDEGAM's threats with violence.
edges.evidence_quote: ACDEGAM worked to promote anti-labor policies and threatened anyone involved with organizing for labor or peasants' rights. The threats were backed by the MAS, which would come in and attack or assassinate anyone who was suspected of being a 'subversive'.

descriptions: MAS collaborated with Cali Cartel and Medellín dissidents to form Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: the Calí Cartel, dissidents within the Medellín Cartel, and the MAS worked together to create a new paramilitary organization known as Perseguidos Por Pablo Escobar

descriptions: Medellín Cartel members co-founded MAS.
edges.evidence_quote: members of the Medellín Cartel, the Colombian military, the U.S.-based corporation Texas Petroleum, the Colombian legislature, small industrialists, and wealthy cattle ranchers came together in a series of meetings in Puerto Boyacá, and formed a paramilitary organization known as Muerte a Secuestradores

descriptions: Medellín Cartel members co-founded MAS.
edges.evidence_quote: Between the end of 1981 and the beginning of 1982, members of the Medellín Cartel, the Colombian military, the U.S.-based corporation Texas Petroleum, the Colombian legislature, small industrialists, and wealthy cattle ranchers came together in a series of meetings in Puerto Boyacá, and formed a paramilitary organization known as Muerte a Secuestradores

descriptions: MAS waged counterinsurgency warfare against FARC-EP.
edges.evidence_quote: Muerte a Secuestradores assassinated political opponents and community organizers, and waged counterinsurgency warfare against guerrilla movements such as the FARC-EP and the M-19.

edges.source: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: ACCU networked with MAS.
edges.evidence_quote: The ACCU began networking with other paramilitary groups such as the MAS

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Armed groups Right-wing paramilitaries , Muerte a Secuestradores

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Muerte a Secuestradores

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Muerte a Secuestradores

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Armed groups Right-wing paramilitaries , Muerte a Secuestradores

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Muerte a Secuestradores

descriptions: Forces of the Fighters of the Tribes members joined SDF.
edges.evidence_quote: dozens of militiamen of the pro-government Forces of the Fighters of the Tribes joined the SDF.

Wilmas 13.description: Wilmas 13 is a Mexican American street gang based in Wilmington, Los Angeles, California, controlled by the Mexican Mafia. It operates through four sub-gangs and is involved in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, murder, and extortion. The gang has been subject to federal racketeering charges and civil injunctions. Its leadership includes Mexican Mafia members directing activities from prison via contraband electronics.

descriptions: Wilmas 13 is controlled by the Mexican Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Wilmas 13 (W13) is a Mexican American gang from Wilmington, Los Angeles , California that is controlled by the Mexican Mafia.

descriptions: Top-level members of IMG Insane MEXICAN Gang report to the Mexican Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: whose low-level members are rivals, top-level members report to the Mexican Mafia

'Ndrina Iannazzo.description: A 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Lamezia Terme, Calabria, Italy. It is allied with the Giampà and Cannizzaro-Daponte clans. The clan has been involved in a long-running feud with the Torcasio-Gualtieri-Cerra clan since 2000, and has been implicated in public contract corruption and extortion. Its members have been targeted in multiple anti-mafia operations, including Effetto Domino, Andromeda, Nettuno, and Quinta Bolgia.

descriptions: Anello and Iannazzo were targeted together in Operation Effetto Domino, and Rocco Anello had good relations with Iannazzo.
edges.evidence_quote: 5 giugno 2008 - Operazione Effetto Domino contro gli Anello- Fruci , i Cerra-Torcasio-Gualtieri , gli Iannazzo e i Passafaro

descriptions: The Fruci and Iannazzo clans were jointly targeted in Operation Effetto Domino, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: 5 giugno 2008 - Operazione "Effetto Domino" contro gli Anello -Fruci, i Cerra-Torcasio-Gualtieri , gli Iannazzo e i Passafaro .

descriptions: Iannazzo is listed as an ally of Giampà in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: Dattilo, Iannazzo

descriptions: Iannazzo is a subunit of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Iannazzo è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese

descriptions: Iannazzo and Cannizzaro-Daponte jointly carried out murders and attempted murders.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 14 maggio 2015 si conclude l'operazione Andromeda ... viene ricostruito l'omicidio del presunto boss Antonio Torcasio del 23 maggio 2003 e il tentato omicidio contro Vincenzo Torcasio ad opera degli Iannazzo e dei Cannizzaro-Daponte.

descriptions: Iannazzo is allied with Cannizzaro-Daponte.
edges.evidence_quote: alleata dei Giampà e Cannizzaro-Daponte

descriptions: Iannazzo and Cannizzaro-Daponte acted together against Torcasio.
edges.evidence_quote: il tentato omicidio contro Vincenzo Torcasio ad opera degli Iannazzo e dei Cannizzaro-Daponte

descriptions: Iannazzo is in a feud with Torcasio-Gualtieri-Cerra.
edges.evidence_quote: faida (faida di Lamezia Terme) che si trascina ancora oggi tra i Torcasio-Gualtieri-Cerra e gli Iannazzo- Giampà

descriptions: Iannazzo and Torcasio-Gualtieri made peace in 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2015 con l'operazione Andromeda viene fatta luce sulla pace tra gli Iannazzo-Giampà ed i Torcasio-Gualtieri avvenuta anche grazie alla mediazione di clan del reggino.

descriptions: Iannazzo lists Dattilo as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: Cannizzaro-Daponte, Pagliuso, Giampà, De Luca, Dattilo

edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2000 scoppia una faida ( faida di Lamezia Terme ) che si trascina ancora oggi tra i Torcasio-Gualtieri-Cerra e gli Iannazzo - Giampà

descriptions: Torcasio made peace with Iannazzo in 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: Viene fatta luce anche sulla pace tra gli Iannazzo - Giampà ed i Torcasio-Gualtieri grazie alla mediazione di clan del reggino

descriptions: The Iranian Call and Reform Organization is described as belonging to the Muslim Brotherhood.
edges.evidence_quote: Iranian Call and Reform Organization, a Sunni Islamist group active in Iran, predominantly among Kurds, has been described as an organization 'that belongs to the Muslim Brotherhood'

Bowery Boys.description: The Bowery Boys were a nativist, anti-Catholic, and anti-Irish criminal gang based in the Bowery neighborhood of Manhattan, New York City, active from the 1830s to the 1860s. Founded by Mike Walsh and William 'Bill the Butcher' Poole, the gang consisted of volunteer firemen and working-class men. They engaged in street fighting, knife fighting, assault, murder, robbery, arson, and rioting, and were known for their long-running feud with the Dead Rabbits and other Five Points gangs.

descriptions: The Atlantic Guards were longtime allies of the Bowery Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: A longtime ally of the Bowery Boys

descriptions: Infobox lists American Guards as allies of Bowery Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: American Guards, Atlantic Guards, Empire Guards, O'Connell Guards, True Blue Americans, American Republican Party (American Nativist Party, American Party), Order of the Star Spangled Banner (Anti-immigrant secret society)

St. Louis crime family.description: The St. Louis crime family was an Italian-American Mafia crime family based in St. Louis, Missouri, active from the 1890s until 2014. It engaged in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, extortion, drug trafficking, and murder. The family had alliances with several other Mafia families and was involved in violent conflicts with rival gangs during Prohibition.

Big Circle Gang.description: Chinese triad formed in the late 1960s in Chinese prison camps by former Red Guards, later relocating to Hong Kong in the 1970s. Known for extreme violence, use of firearms, and sophisticated criminal operations including drug trafficking, human trafficking, and racketeering. Active internationally in Canada, the United States, Australia, and Malaysia.
